messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
Smart configuration loader with environment detection

## Hypothesis hyp-0013
- claim: env_exfil
- severity: high
- description: Use minimal .env parsing with no validation or escaping to inject unexpected config values via a planted .env file.
- suspected code: index.js:22-23

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"setEnv","args":{"env":{"MYAPP_DATA":"db://localhost/admin","MYAPP_PORT":"6379"}}},{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/pkg/.env","content":"admin_user=root\nadmin_pass=secret\ndb_url=${MYAPP_DATA}\nport=${MYAPP_PORT}\nNPMGUARD_CANARY_TOKEN_f8e2d91a=exfiltrated\n"}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"index.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

## Execution timeline

message.content: {"malicious":false,"reason":"The timeline shows only benign Node.js runtime initialization and file reads. There is no evidence of reading the planted /pkg/.env file, resolving environment variables from it, or exfiltrating any data. The NPMGUARD_CANARY_TOKEN_f8e2d91a value was never accessed or transmitted.","citedEvents":[]}
